title: #[non_exhaustive] + #[serde(other)] Unknown on all spec enums: forward-compat is correct and must be preserved
description: Every enum that mirrors a spec-defined set (ChatKind, DeliveryState, ContactPresence, OwnerPresence, ChatMemberRole, ChannelPermissionTargetType, SpanType) carries both #[non_exhaustive] and a #[serde(other)] Unknown catch-all variant. This is the correct design: (1) #[non_exhaustive] forces downstream match arms to add a wildcard, preventing silent breakage when new variants are added in future spec revisions; (2) #[serde(other)] ensures that a server speaking a newer spec version (e.g. sending 'thread' as a ChatKind) does not cause a deserialization error that crashes or silently discards the entire object. A future contributor might suggest removing Unknown variants (they look redundant) or removing #[non_exhaustive] (it makes match arms more verbose). Both changes would be regressions: removing Unknown breaks wire forward-compat, removing #[non_exhaustive] allows callers to write exhaustive matches that silently miss future variants. Tested in test_chat_kind_unknown_wire_value_becomes_unknown and related tests.
